title: Generalisable tissue-wide molecular reconstruction from histology
categories: histology-based prediction; tissue-wide reconstruction; spatial transcriptomics
summary: Predicting tissue-wide gene expression from routine H&E histology when spatial transcriptomics is sparse, partial, and heterogeneous across tissue samples. GHIST+ combines cellular morphology, local context, and shared tissue representations to reconstruct biologically meaningful molecular maps from TMA-derived data across cancers and GTEx breast tissues, preserving spatial structure and cell-type organization.

title: Depletion of NAT10 in T cells attenuates metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis in mice.
categories: liver disease; t cell immunology; rna modification
summary: In MASH, intrahepatic effector CD8+ T cells show elevated NAT10; T cell-specific NAT10 knockout in mice reduced hepatocyte injury, fibrosis, and CD8+ T-cell accumulation by suppressing IFN-γ/TNF-α/GZMB via ac4C-mediated stabilization of Fhit mRNA.

title: IL15-mediated crosstalk between LSECs and CD8⁺ tissue-resident memory T cells drives alcohol-associated hepatitis.
categories: cell-cell communication; t cell immunology; single-cell transcriptomics
summary: Defined how CD8 tissue-resident memory T cells drive alcohol-associated hepatitis using murine chronic-binge/LPS models, human single-cell data, and LSEC co-culture. IL15 from the hepatic niche amplified LSEC–CD8 signaling via PI3K-AKT; IL15 blockade reduced TRM abundance and attenuated liver injury.

title: Genome-wide association and multi-omics functional screens reveal the genetic architecture of foveal development
categories: developmental genetics; genome-wide association; multi-omics integration
summary: GWAS plus developmental multi-omics for foveal hypoplasia identified 42 sentinel variants mapping to 54 effector genes, with CRISPR/mutant zebrafish knockouts of six genes reproducing foveal-hypoplasia phenotypes. Human fetal single-cell/spatial data showed two expression waves and Müller glia as key patterning mediators, linking foveal variants to refractive, lenticular, and metabolic traits.

title: Single-Cell Studies Advance Understanding of the Genetic and Molecular Basis of Atherosclerosis.
summary: Single-cell and spatial genomics are used to dissect atherosclerotic plaque heterogeneity, linking genetic risk to specific vascular and immune cell states and regulatory programs. The review highlights that scRNA-seq/scATAC-seq and spatial transcriptomics have revealed intermediate smooth muscle/endothelial phenotypes and plaque-localized signaling, but gives no single headline result.

title: Dual Developmental Origins and Activity-dependent Specification of Mammalian Subplate Neurons
categories: cortical development; neuronal cell fate; neocortical evolution
summary: Comparative single-cell/spatial transcriptomics across mice, chicks, and turtles reveals two subplate neuron origins: conserved Nr4a2-negative atypical SpNs from medial pallium and mammal-expanded Nr4a2-positive mSpNs from early-born cortical neurons. Zbtb18 repression and thalamic input drive the mammalian fate switch.

title: A DPHV-liver module recapitulates AML infiltration and chemotherapy-induced hepatotoxicity with translational utility.
categories: liver disease modeling; vascularized organoid model; drug-induced hepatotoxicity
summary: Perfusable human vascularized liver module models AML liver infiltration and cytarabine hepatotoxicity using primary hepatocytes; drug screening identified ammonium glycyrrhizinate as reducing hepatotoxicity and leukemic infiltration without weakening antileukemic efficacy, with trends matching a retrospective clinical cohort.

title: MICA/MICB-Mediated NKG2D Immune Escape in Cervical Cancer: Single-Cell Transcriptomic Mapping of Radionuclide Therapy Targets for Precision Radioimmunotherapy.
categories: single-cell transcriptomics; cervical cancer; radionuclide therapy
summary: Mapped NKG2D-ligand immune escape in cervical cancer using scRNA-seq and qPCR/ELISA validation to assess radionuclide therapy targets. Found an immune-cold TME with depleted CD8+ T/NK cells, reduced MICA/MICB (HeLa MICA 0.44×, MICB 0.51×), and increased SUSD1 (2.28×), predicting poor precision RNT targeting.

title: Biological Aging of the Cardiopulmonary System
categories: biological aging; cardiopulmonary system; single-cell transcriptomics
summary: Maps cardiopulmonary aging in mice by combining RV, proximal pulmonary artery, and lung biomechanics with single-cell transcriptomics; PCA-derived physiology-based aging scores better capture organ aging than chronological age. Pulmonary artery stiffening rose nearly linearly, while RV/lung changes were nonlinear, and aging scores revealed 13,636 cell type-specific gene changes linked to oxidative phosphorylation and loss of adaptive signaling.

title: RAI1 safeguards fidelity and tempo of human neurodevelopmental gene expression
categories: human neurodevelopment; developmental timing; gene expression regulation
summary: Using isogenic RAI1 loss-of-function human stem cells and longitudinal/single-cell RNA-seq during cortical differentiation, the study tests how RAI1 shapes neurodevelopmental gene regulation. RAI1 loss ускорates developmental progression, induces a transient mesoderm-like program, and precociously activates synaptic genes, identifying RAI1 as a brake on human neurodevelopmental tempo.
